qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SSE Event ID and Type Field CRLF Injection
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes CRLF injection in SSE event serialization (event.go:32, event.go:39) due to unsanitized id/type fields. The advisory describes a cache key collision in TopicSelectorStore due to underscore-separated string concatenation. These are completely different vulnerable components and root causes.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Missing Authorization on Subscription Endpoints in Anonymous Mode
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes missing authorization checks on subscription API endpoints (subscription.go:123-131) when anonymous mode is enabled. The advisory describes a cache key collision in TopicSelectorStore due to underscore-separated string concatenation. These are different vulnerable components and root causes.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The advisory describes a cache key collision vulnerability in TopicSelectorStore where cache keys were constructed via string concatenation with an underscore separator ("m_" + topicSelector + "_" + topic), allowing distinct selector/topic pairs to produce identical keys. Neither qualifying finding identifies this root cause. sse-crlf-injection addresses unsanitized CRLF in SSE event fields (event.go). subscription-info-disclosure addresses missing authorization on subscription endpoints (subscription.go).
evidence: Advisory root cause: cache key collision from underscore-separated string concatenation in TopicSelectorStore (topicselector.go)
sse-crlf-injection: different component (event.go SSE serialization) and different root cause (CRLF injection, not cache key collision)
subscription-info-disclosure: different component (subscription.go authorization) and different root cause (missing auth check in anonymous mode, not cache key collision)
